title: Gantt chart quarters are misaligned to years
excerpt:

posts.author: julian.irwin (Julian)
posts.cookedHtml:
Has anyone else found that Quarters and Years are misaligned in the Gantt view for certain zoom levels? Is there a known workaround?

I can’t present this chart to others in my organization with this bug in place, it makes the chart look messy and confusing.

Link to screenshot of bug

posts.author: ZakiImtiaz (Zaki | readylogic.co)
posts.cookedHtml:
Here are a few potential workarounds you could try:

  1. Adjust the zoom level - sometimes changing to a different zoom level (either zooming in or out) can fix the alignment issue
  2. Try a different time scale display option - Monday.com usually offers different ways to display the timeline (months only, years only, etc.)
  3. Export to another tool - If you need to present this, you could export the data to another project management tool or even recreate the key parts in PowerPoint/Google Slides where you have more control over the visual alignment
  4. Report the bug - Make sure to report this to Monday.com’s support team, as they may have a known fix or could add it to their bug tracking system

Zaki
Business Workflow Consultant
Ready Logic

posts.author: Mollyverance (Mollyverance)
posts.cookedHtml: i noticed the same thing and just ended up manually adjusting dates to match the visual quarters. Not ideal, but it worked okay for getting reports out the door fast

posts.author: Mollyverance (Mollyverance)
posts.cookedHtml:
i noticed the same thing and just ended up manually adjusting dates to match the visual quarters. Not ideal, but it worked okay for getting reports out the door fast

it threw off my whole timeline during a sprint review. I had to present our roadmap in PowerPoint, and the misaligned quarters made it look way more chaotic than it really was. What helped was switching tools to make Gantt chart online instead—it gave me cleaner exports and the timeline stayed aligned across zoom levels.
